result.stdout:
Description:
  A2A-Ask: Interact with A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol agents from the command line

Usage:
  a2a-ask [command] [options]

Options:
  --output <json|text>  Output format [default: json]
  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  --version             Show version information
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

Commands:
  discover <url>  Fetch and display an A2A agent card
  send <url>      Send a message to an A2A agent
  stream <url>    Send a message with streaming response, or subscribe to task events
  task            Manage A2A tasks
  auth            Authentication management
  version         Display A2A-Ask version information

commands.payload:

  send <url>      Send a message to an A2A agent

result.stdout:
Description:
  Authentication management

Usage:
  a2a-ask auth [command] [options]

Options:
  --output <json|text>  Output format [default: json]
  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

Commands:
  login <url>   Interactively authenticate with an A2A agent
  logout <url>  Remove stored authentication token for an agent
  status <url>  Show authentication status for an agent

commands.payload:

  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]

Commands:

  logout <url>  Remove stored authentication token for an agent

result.stdout:
Description:
  Interactively authenticate with an A2A agent

Usage:
  a2a-ask auth login <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent base URL to authenticate against

Options:
  --client-id <client-id>          OAuth2 client ID for client_credentials grant
  --client-secret <client-secret>  OAuth2 client secret for client_credentials grant
  --tenant <tenant>                Tenant ID
  --output <json|text>             Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                         Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                    Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Fetch and display an A2A agent card

Usage:
  a2a-ask discover <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Base URL of the agent (or direct agent card URL)

Options:
  --well-known                 Append /.well-known/agent-card.json to the URL [default: True]
  --extended                   Fetch the extended (authenticated) agent card [default: False]
  --auth-token <auth-token>    Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>  Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --tenant <tenant>            Tenant ID
  --output <json|text>         Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                     Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Fetch and display an A2A agent card

Usage:
  a2a-ask discover <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Base URL of the agent (or direct agent card URL)

Options:
  --well-known                 Append /.well-known/agent-card.json to the URL [default: True]
  --extended                   Fetch the extended (authenticated) agent card [default: False]
  --auth-token <auth-token>    Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>  Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --tenant <tenant>            Tenant ID
  --output <json|text>         Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                     Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Send a message to an A2A agent

Usage:
  a2a-ask send <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent endpoint URL

Options:
  -m, --message <message>            Message text to send
  -f, --file <file>                  File to include as a message part
  -d, --data <data>                  Structured JSON data to include as a message part
  -t, --task-id <task-id>            Task ID for continuing an existing task
  -c, --context-id <context-id>      Context ID for grouping related interactions
  --message-id <message-id>          Custom message ID (defaults to auto-generated UUID)
  --accept <accept>                  Accepted output modes (comma-separated media types)
  --return-immediately               Return immediately without waiting for task completion [default: False]
  --history-length <history-length>  Maximum number of history messages to include in response
  --auth-token <auth-token>          Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>        Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --api-key <api-key>                API key value
  --api-key-header <api-key-header>  API key header name (defaults to agent card setting)
  --auth-user <auth-user>            Username for HTTP Basic authentication
  --auth-password <auth-password>    Password for HTTP Basic authentication
  --client-id <client-id>            OAuth2 client ID for client_credentials grant
  --client-secret <client-secret>    OAuth2 client secret for client_credentials grant
  --binding <binding>                Protocol binding: auto, http, jsonrpc [default: auto]
  --a2a-version <a2a-version>        A2A protocol version [default: 1.0]
  --tenant <tenant>                  Tenant ID
  --save-artifacts <save-artifacts>  Directory to save file artifacts to disk
  --output <json|text>               Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                           Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                      Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Send a message to an A2A agent

Usage:
  a2a-ask send <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent endpoint URL

Options:
  -m, --message <message>            Message text to send
  -f, --file <file>                  File to include as a message part
  -d, --data <data>                  Structured JSON data to include as a message part
  -t, --task-id <task-id>            Task ID for continuing an existing task

  --history-length <history-length>  Maximum number of history messages to include in response

  --auth-header <auth-header>        Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --api-key <api-key>                API key value
  --api-key-header <api-key-header>  API key header name (defaults to agent card setting)
  --auth-user <auth-user>            Username for HTTP Basic authentication
  --auth-password <auth-password>    Password for HTTP Basic authentication
  --client-id <client-id>            OAuth2 client ID for client_credentials grant
  --client-secret <client-secret>    OAuth2 client secret for client_credentials grant
  --binding <binding>                Protocol binding: auto, http, jsonrpc [default: auto]
  --a2a-version <a2a-version>        A2A protocol version [default: 1.0]
  --tenant <tenant>                  Tenant ID
  --save-artifacts <save-artifacts>  Directory to save file artifacts to disk

  -v, --verbose                      Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Send a message with streaming response, or subscribe to task events

Usage:
  a2a-ask stream <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent endpoint URL

Options:
  -m, --message <message>            Message text to send
  -f, --file <file>                  File to include as a message part
  -d, --data <data>                  Structured JSON data part
  --subscribe                        Subscribe to an existing task's events (requires --task-id) [default: False]
  -t, --task-id <task-id>            Task ID for continuing an existing task
  -c, --context-id <context-id>      Context ID for grouping related interactions
  --accept <accept>                  Accepted output modes (comma-separated)
  --history-length <history-length>  Max history messages
  --auth-token <auth-token>          Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>        Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --api-key <api-key>                API key value
  --api-key-header <api-key-header>  API key header name (defaults to agent card setting)
  --auth-user <auth-user>            Username for HTTP Basic authentication
  --auth-password <auth-password>    Password for HTTP Basic authentication
  --client-id <client-id>            OAuth2 client ID for client_credentials grant
  --client-secret <client-secret>    OAuth2 client secret for client_credentials grant
  --tenant <tenant>                  Tenant ID
  --save-artifacts <save-artifacts>  Directory to save file artifacts to disk
  --output <json|text>               Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                           Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                      Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage A2A tasks

Usage:
  a2a-ask task [command] [options]

Options:
  --output <json|text>  Output format [default: json]
  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

Commands:
  get <url>     Get the current state of a task
  list <url>    List tasks with optional filtering
  cancel <url>  Cancel a running task

commands.payload:
Description:

  list <url>    List tasks with optional filtering
  cancel <url>  Cancel a running task

result.stdout:
Description:
  Cancel a running task

Usage:
  a2a-ask task cancel <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent endpoint URL

Options:
  -t, --task-id <task-id> (REQUIRED)  Task ID to cancel
  --auth-token <auth-token>           Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>         Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --api-key <api-key>                 API key value
  --api-key-header <api-key-header>   API key header name (defaults to agent card setting)
  --auth-user <auth-user>             Username for HTTP Basic authentication
  --auth-password <auth-password>     Password for HTTP Basic authentication
  --tenant <tenant>                   Tenant ID
  --output <json|text>                Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                            Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                       Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Get the current state of a task

Usage:
  a2a-ask task get <url> [options]

Arguments:

Options:
  -t, --task-id <task-id> (REQUIRED)  Task ID
  --history-length <history-length>   Max history messages to include
  --auth-token <auth-token>           Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>         Custom auth header (key=value format)

result.stdout:
Description:
  List tasks with optional filtering

Usage:
  a2a-ask task list <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  Agent endpoint URL

Options:
  --context-id <context-id>          Filter by context ID
  --status <status>                  Filter by task state
  --page-size <page-size>            Results per page (default: 50)
  --page-token <page-token>          Pagination cursor token
  --auth-token <auth-token>          Bearer token for authentication
  --auth-header <auth-header>        Custom auth header (key=value format)
  --api-key <api-key>                API key value
  --api-key-header <api-key-header>  API key header name (defaults to agent card setting)
  --auth-user <auth-user>            Username for HTTP Basic authentication
  --auth-password <auth-password>    Password for HTTP Basic authentication
  --tenant <tenant>                  Tenant ID
  --output <json|text>               Output format [default: json]
  --pretty                           Pretty-print JSON output [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                      Enable verbose/debug output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information
